meta.description: 30 lightweight multi-agent benchmark tasks (2-3 agents each) for AgentGlue coordination and cache evaluation
meta.design: 10 groups of 3 tasks testing different multi-agent coordination patterns. Each task spawns 2-3 sub-agents with overlapping file reads and searches.

tasks.name: Runtime public API vs private helpers
tasks.description: 2 agents read runtime.py — one lists public methods, the other lists private helpers
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/runtime.py and list all public methods (no leading underscore) with their signatures.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/runtime.py and list all private/internal methods (leading underscore) with their purpose.
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Both agents read runtime.py; second agent should get cache hit.

tasks.name: Dedup class hierarchy vs error handling
tasks.description: 2 agents read dedup.py — one maps class hierarchy, the other checks error handling
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/dedup.py and map the class hierarchy (which classes inherit from what).
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/dedup.py and list all try/except blocks and what errors they handle.
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Both agents read dedup.py.

tasks.name: Shared memory read vs write paths
tasks.description: 2 agents read shared_memory.py — one traces read path, the other traces write path
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/shared_memory.py and trace the data read path from entry to return.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/shared_memory.py and trace the data write path from entry to storage.
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Both agents read shared_memory.py.

tasks.name: Search 'async' in middleware vs full src
tasks.description: 2 agents search for 'async' — one in middleware/, one in all of src/
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Search for the pattern 'async' in /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/ and count occurrences per file.
- Agent 2: Search for the pattern 'async' in /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/ and count occurrences per file.
Report combined findings noting the overlap.
tasks.notes: middleware/ is inside src/agentglue/, so agent 2's search is a superset of agent 1's.

tasks.name: Search 'import' in core vs full src
tasks.description: 2 agents search for 'import' — one in core/, one in all of src/
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Search for 'import' statements in /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/core/ and list all external dependencies.
- Agent 2: Search for 'import' statements in /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/ and list all external dependencies.
Report combined findings noting the overlap.
tasks.notes: core/ is inside src/agentglue/, so results overlap.

tasks.name: Search 'sqlite' in middleware vs full repo
tasks.description: 2 agents search for 'sqlite' — one in middleware/, one in full repo
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Search for 'sqlite' (case-insensitive) in /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/ and list all SQLite-related code.
- Agent 2: Search for 'sqlite' (case-insensitive) in /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/ and list all SQLite-related code.
Report combined findings noting the overlap.
tasks.notes: middleware/ is a subset of the full repo.

tasks.name: Parallel read of three middleware files
tasks.description: 3 agents each read a different middleware file
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 3 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/dedup.py and summarize its purpose in 2 sentences.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/shared_memory.py and summarize its purpose in 2 sentences.
- Agent 3: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/task_lock.py and summarize its purpose in 2 sentences.
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Different files in same module; agents may read __init__.py as common import.

tasks.name: Parallel read of three core files
tasks.description: 3 agents each read a different core file
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 3 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/core/allocator.py and list its main class and methods.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/core/events.py and list its main class and methods.
- Agent 3: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/core/metrics.py and list its main class and methods.
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Different files in core/ module; agents may share __init__.py reads.

tasks.name: Parallel read of core recorder, events, allocator
tasks.description: 3 agents read recorder.py, events.py, and allocator.py
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 3 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/core/recorder.py and describe what data it records.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/core/events.py and describe the event types it defines.
- Agent 3: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/core/allocator.py and describe the allocation strategy.
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Three core files; likely shared imports.

tasks.name: Runtime imports dedup — interface check
tasks.description: 2 agents check that runtime.py and dedup.py agree on interfaces
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/runtime.py and list every call it makes into the dedup middleware, noting the arguments passed.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/dedup.py and list the signature of every public method.
Report whether the call sites match the signatures.
tasks.notes: Agents may both read runtime.py or dedup.py to cross-reference.

tasks.name: Runtime imports shared_memory — interface check
tasks.description: 2 agents check that runtime.py and shared_memory.py agree on interfaces
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/runtime.py and list every call it makes into shared_memory, noting arguments.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/shared_memory.py and list the signature of every public method.
Report whether the call sites match the signatures.
tasks.notes: Cross-reference between runtime and shared_memory.

tasks.name: Runtime imports task_lock — interface check
tasks.description: 2 agents check that runtime.py and task_lock.py agree on interfaces
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/runtime.py and list every call it makes to task_lock functionality, noting arguments.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/task_lock.py and list every public method signature.
Report whether the call sites match the signatures.
tasks.notes: Cross-reference between runtime and task_lock.

tasks.name: Dedup security vs performance review
tasks.description: 2 agents review dedup.py — one for security, one for performance
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/dedup.py and review it for security issues (SQL injection, path traversal, unsafe deserialization).
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/dedup.py and review it for performance issues (unnecessary copies, O(n^2) loops, blocking I/O).
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Both agents read the same file from different angles.

tasks.name: Shared memory security vs performance review
tasks.description: 2 agents review shared_memory.py — one for security, one for performance
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/shared_memory.py and review for security issues (race conditions, data leaks, unauthorized access).
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/shared_memory.py and review for performance issues (memory waste, slow lookups, excessive locking).
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Both agents read shared_memory.py.

tasks.name: Task lock security vs performance review
tasks.description: 2 agents review task_lock.py — one for security, one for performance
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/task_lock.py and review for security issues (deadlocks, lock starvation, privilege escalation).
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/task_lock.py and review for performance issues (contention, unnecessary lock scope, blocking waits).
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Both agents read task_lock.py.

tasks.name: Search async in middleware, then read dedup
tasks.description: One agent searches, one reads; search results overlap with read target
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Search for 'async def' in /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/ and list all async functions found.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/dedup.py and list all function definitions.
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Agent 1's search will read dedup.py (among others), overlapping with agent 2's direct read.

tasks.name: Search class in core, then read allocator
tasks.description: One agent searches core/ for classes, one reads allocator.py directly
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Search for 'class ' in /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/core/ and list all classes found.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/core/allocator.py and describe its class hierarchy.
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Search results will include allocator.py, overlapping with the direct read.

tasks.name: Search import in src, then read runtime
tasks.description: One agent searches for imports, one reads runtime.py
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Search for 'from agentglue' in /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/ and map the internal import graph.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/runtime.py and list all its import statements.
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Search will read runtime.py as part of results.

tasks.name: Summarize middleware module — 3 agents
tasks.description: 3 agents each summarize one middleware file, all reading the module's __init__.py
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 3 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/dedup.py and summarize the dedup middleware's role. Also read the middleware __init__.py if it exists.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/shared_memory.py and summarize the shared memory middleware's role. Also read the middleware __init__.py if it exists.
- Agent 3: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/task_lock.py and summarize the task lock middleware's role. Also read the middleware __init__.py if it exists.
Report combined findings as a unified middleware overview.
tasks.notes: All 3 agents read __init__.py as common entry; high overlap on that file.

tasks.name: Summarize core module — 3 agents
tasks.description: 3 agents each summarize one core file, all reading the module's __init__.py
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 3 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/core/allocator.py and summarize the allocator's role. Also read the core __init__.py if it exists.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/core/events.py and summarize the event system's role. Also read the core __init__.py if it exists.
- Agent 3: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/core/metrics.py and summarize the metrics system's role. Also read the core __init__.py if it exists.
Report combined findings as a unified core module overview.
tasks.notes: All 3 agents read core __init__.py.

tasks.name: Summarize core observability — recorder, metrics, events
tasks.description: 3 agents summarize observability files with shared __init__.py
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 3 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/core/recorder.py and explain how recording integrates with the core module. Also read the core __init__.py.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/core/metrics.py and explain how metrics integrate with the core module. Also read the core __init__.py.
- Agent 3: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/core/events.py and explain how events integrate with the core module. Also read the core __init__.py.
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: All 3 agents read core __init__.py; overlap on shared types.

tasks.name: Test vs source — dedup middleware
tasks.description: 2 agents: one reads dedup source, one reads tests; both understand dedup module
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/dedup.py and list all public methods that should be tested.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/tests/test_sqlite_backend.py and list all test functions and what dedup behavior they verify.
Report whether all public methods have test coverage.
tasks.notes: Both agents need to understand dedup; agent 2 may read dedup.py too.

tasks.name: Test vs source — sidecar server
tasks.description: 2 agents: one reads server source, one reads sidecar benchmark tests
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/openclaw-agentglue/sidecar/server.py and list all endpoints that should be tested.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/tests/test_sidecar_benchmark.py and list all test functions and what server behavior they verify.
Report whether all endpoints have test coverage.
tasks.notes: Both agents need to understand the sidecar server.

tasks.name: Test vs source — runtime module
tasks.description: 2 agents: one reads runtime source, one searches for runtime tests
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/runtime.py and list all public methods that need tests.
- Agent 2: Search for 'runtime' in /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/tests/ and identify which runtime methods are tested.
Report coverage gaps.
tasks.notes: Agent 2 may need to read runtime.py to understand what it's looking for.

tasks.name: Config + code — pyproject.toml vs runtime imports
tasks.description: 2 agents: one reads pyproject.toml deps, one reads runtime.py imports
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/pyproject.toml and list all declared dependencies.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/runtime.py and list all third-party imports.
Report whether all imported packages are declared in pyproject.toml.
tasks.notes: Both agents need to understand dependencies; may cross-read each other's file.

tasks.name: Config + code — package.json vs index.ts imports
tasks.description: 2 agents: one reads package.json deps, one reads index.ts imports
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/openclaw-agentglue/package.json and list all declared dependencies.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/openclaw-agentglue/src/index.ts and list all import/require statements.
Report whether all imported packages are declared in package.json.
tasks.notes: Both agents share dependency context.

tasks.name: Config + code — pyproject.toml version vs README version
tasks.description: 2 agents: one reads pyproject.toml for version, one reads README for version mentions
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/pyproject.toml and find the project version and Python version requirements.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/README.md and find any version numbers or compatibility claims mentioned.
Report whether version info is consistent.
tasks.notes: Both agents analyze version-related information.

tasks.name: Full overlap — runtime.py three questions
tasks.description: 3 agents all read runtime.py and answer different questions
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 3 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/runtime.py and count the total lines of code, classes, and functions.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/runtime.py and list all decorator usages.
- Agent 3: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/runtime.py and identify all global variables and module-level constants.
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: All 3 agents read the exact same file — maximum cache hit potential.

tasks.name: Full overlap — dedup.py + shared_memory.py two questions
tasks.description: 2 agents both read dedup.py and shared_memory.py, different analyses
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/dedup.py and /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/shared_memory.py. Describe how data flows between them.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/dedup.py and /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/shared_memory.py. Identify any potential race conditions between them.
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Both agents read the exact same 2 files.

tasks.name: Full overlap — server.py + index.ts two questions
tasks.description: 2 agents both read server.py and index.ts, different analyses
tasks.prompt:
Spawn exactly 2 sub-agents in parallel:
- Agent 1: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/openclaw-agentglue/sidecar/server.py and /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/openclaw-agentglue/src/index.ts. Describe the communication protocol between them.
- Agent 2: Read /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/openclaw-agentglue/sidecar/server.py and /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/openclaw-agentglue/src/index.ts. Identify any error handling gaps in their interaction.
Report combined findings.
tasks.notes: Both agents read the exact same 2 files.
